mode_reason: Pre-flight Operator Directive Discovery returned 1 pending: DIRECTIVE-20260505-003 (priority=medium, target_mode=Mode 4). Directive overrides Mode 1-4 trigger logic per the dispatcher's Pre-flight rule.
previous_followup: Skipped this run per directive scope (directives override the normal Mode 1-4 flow). Next non-directive Mode 4 run resumes PROP lifecycle verification.

findings.description: Curmudgeon change-detection (Priority 3) silently misroutes pre-tldr-schema fingerprints into the lowest priority bucket, stranding ~22 high-drift WINs whose latest review predates the 2026-04-09 tldr_*_length schema addition. Directive operator's hypothesis was NaN propagation in the drift comparison; empirical trace confirms the symptom but identifies the actual mechanism as bucket-misrouting (Step 4 prioritization classifies items with missing-tldr-old as 'TLDR-only changes' even when the dominant drift signal is on a legacy field). Fleet-wide, 146 of 164 distinct review items carry pre-tldr fingerprints; the bug class will repeat on every future schema addition.
findings.evidence: 14 deep-tail c2 review files all share the legacy 5-field fingerprint; current data has 7-field schema. Drift on the four legacy fields (computed this run): WIN-050 vd +838%, WIN-028 vd +837%, WIN-009 vd +638%, WIN-036 vd +636%, WIN-060 vd +609%, WIN-018 vd +514%, WIN-059 vd +467%, WIN-057 vd +372%, WIN-023 vd +334%, WIN-048 vd +333%, WIN-063 vd +258%, WIN-006 vd +249%, WIN-043 vd +248%, WIN-066 vd +243%. None reviewed in 28 days. Wider population: 22 of 24 c1/c2-latest WINs with missing-tldr fingerprints have legacy-field drift exceeding 20% or a verdict change.

suggested_fix.replace: Reduced-set comparison: compare on the intersection of fingerprint fields; schema additions are not a drift signal in themselves; classify by the field that drifted, never by 'tldr was added'. See PROP-019 for full text.
suggested_fix.rationale: Routes the deep tail through the correct existing bucket without introducing new architecture; resilient to future schema additions.

directive_outcome.propose_only_compliance: no edits to monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md, monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md, or any review files. No priority-queue pushes. PROP-019 awaits operator authorization.

recommendations.description: Review and authorize PROP-019 (curmudgeon change-detection reduced-set comparison fix). Recommended option is option 2 of the three laid out; operator must decide between options before any prompt edit lands.

Decide whether to bundle the optional follow-ups with the PROP-019 implementation: (a) add fingerprint_schema_version field to canonical schema in monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md, (b) add tinker Mode 3 audit step that counts items with outdated fingerprint shapes. Both are strictly preventive; not blocking PROP-019 itself.

Confirm operator does NOT want a one-time priority-queue push of the 14 deep-tail items. PROP-019 argues against it: queue-pushing the deep tail would conflate channels and obscure whether the structural fix actually works. Operator's directive lean was 'unhurried structural fix' — PROP-019 aligns.
